goal: Ship subcontractor a reproducible package: scene + checkpoint + one-command run script + sanity check.
thoughts: Two past 'works on my machine' failures — this one must pin everything. export_scene_package with relative refs. Ship checkpoint separately via signed URL (2GB bloats the zip). Sanity-check script loads scene, steps physics 10x, prints an observation hash — matches = env reproducible, mismatches = investigate before writing code. Pin Isaac Sim version in README.
code:
# 1. Audit refs before packing
run_stage_analysis()

# 2. Pack scene (not checkpoint)
export_scene_package(
    stage_path="<STAGE_USD>",
    output_path="<HANDOFF_ZIP>",
    format="zip",
    rewrite_refs_relative=True,
    include_textures=True,
    isaac_sim_version="<5.1.0>"
)

# 3. Sanity-check script — subcontractor runs this first
run_usd_script(
    description="Generate reproducibility check script",
    code="""
# reproducibility_check.py — expected SHA at bottom
import hashlib, json, omni.usd
stage = omni.usd.get_context().open_stage('<RELATIVE_STAGE_PATH>')
for _ in range(10):
    omni.kit.commands.execute('Simulate')
obs = get_env_observations(env_id=0)
print('OBS_HASH', hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(obs, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest())
# EXPECTED: <RAJS_LOCAL_HASH>
"""
)

# 4. Ship checkpoint via signed URL in README, NOT in zip
failure_modes: Absolute paths from Raj's home dir leak into run script
Windows subcontractor hits path-separator issues — always use pathlib
Checkpoint inside zip balloons it to 2GB+
Sanity check depends on deterministic physics without pinning timestep/seed
